text: Setting clear goals is essential for any project’s success. A useful method is to create SMART goals: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” you could set a SMART goal like “increase site traffic by 20% within three months.” To stay on track, break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing a design review each week. It is also important to define deliverables—tangible outcomes like a report or a prototype. Finally, stakeholder alignment means checking that everyone involved agrees on these goals. This avoids confusion later and helps the team move forward together.
